page: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core.

This server includes 2835 GB of bundled local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not feature any GPUs. The AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

In benchmark testing, the D48ads_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3680.45 and a gzip compression rate of 41.21 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10%. Multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% with a stress-ng multi-core score of 86468.87. Memory bandwidth tests show a strong cached read speed of 4950.45 GB/sec and a large-block write speed of 94.13 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read bandwidth is average at 157.02 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D48ads_v7 offers a balanced resource density, pairing high-frequency dedicated compute cores with substantial local storage and memory. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads, database hosting, large-scale file processing, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM and 2835 GB of local storage. The architecture supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions. In benchmark evaluations, the server achieves top-tier single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests and gzip compression. Multi-core performance and large-block write memory bandwidth are also strong, placing in the top 25%. Lacking a GPU, this instance relies entirely on its x86_64 CPU resources. The balance of dedicated compute, high-speed memory, and substantial local storage makes this server suitable for database hosting, enterprise applications, and data compression workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processors, substantial local storage, and top-tier single-core performance.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
2835 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance ranking in the top 10% on stress-ng benchmarks
Strong multi-core capabilities and high-speed memory write bandwidth
Suited for database hosting, enterprise applications, and compression tasks
